title: Iceberg Rest Catalog: DB::Exception: Unable to parse ExceptionName: InvalidRequest Message
description:
## Current Behaviour
I am creating a database in clickhouse with the following command:
```sql
CREATE DATABASE iceberg_dev
ENGINE = DataLakeCatalog('https://my-rest-catalog')
SETTINGS
catalog_type = 'rest',
storage_endpoint = 'http://my_s3_endpoint:port',
warehouse = 's3://iceberg-warehouse/',
vended_credentials = 0,
aws_access_key_id = 's3_key',
aws_secret_access_key = 's3_secret';
```
The storage endpoint in this case is an on prem s3 compliant storage system, and I'm sure the access and secret key are correct.
Attempting to query a table within the database
```sql
select * from iceberg_dev.\"test.foo\"
```
I get the error:
```
DB::Exception: Unable to parse ExceptionName: InvalidRequest Message: Error creating s3 auth info.
The request may not be signed using AWS V4 signing algorithm, or might be invalid. (S3_ERROR)
(version 25.7.1.3997 (official build))
```
I'm not entirely sure what the error means...
---
## Additional Context
Reading the file from the s3 endpoint directly works fine:
```sql
select *
FROM s3('http://my_s3_endpoint:port/x/y/z.parquet',  's3_key', 's3_secret','Parquet')
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE iceberg_dev.test_foo (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, value FLOAT);
INSERT INTO iceberg_dev.test_foo (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'test1', 10.5), (2, 'test2', NULL), (3, 'test3', 20.0);
SELECT id, name, value, created_at FROM iceberg_dev.test_foo WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM iceberg_dev.test_foo WHERE created_at > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1' DAY;
key_bug_features: Invalid S3 authentication during database query execution
